messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "som-window-semantics-year1-zero",
 "title": "The 1% Year-1 anchor is a 36-month accrual read as an annual snapshot — the pre-launch clock makes even that generous",
 "narrative": "obtainableFactor's 1% was 'corrected' in refinement cycle 1 and now looks conservative and triangulated. But the benchmark corpus it leans on defines SOM as a 1–3 year (Prospeo, IdeaPlan) or 3–5 year (Wise) cumulative capture, not a Year-1 run-rate, and the one worked example in the corpus lands at 0.1% of SAM — 5x below the band's own floor. Layer the venture's actual clock on top: it is pre-launch, so months 1–6 are certification, listing and pipeline-building, not selling. A 'conservative ramp' that books its first PO in month 7–9 doesn't earn 1% of SAM; it earns a fraction of the back half of the year. The band's low edge (0.5%) is not the pessimistic case — it is the optimistic reading of the sources' own semantics.",
 "category": "fact",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "The Prospeo/IdeaPlan/Wise benchmark family defines the 1–5% obtainable share as accruing over 1–3 or 3–5 years, not per-year; the ledger pins 1% strictly to Year 1",
   "refs": [
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c1",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c5",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c6",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The only concrete worked example in the corpus yields SOM/SAM ≈ 0.1% ($3.2M/$3.2B) for years 1–2, and the Tractian comparable started at $200K — not the ~$1.2M first-year figure the ledger's crossCheck asserts",
   "refs": [
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c7",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c10"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The venture is pre-launch with a conservative-ramp profile: the selling window inside Year 1 is 6–9 months at best, so any annualized benchmark must be pro-rated before it is applied",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Stage",
    "profile:What obtainable horizon should Year-1 (YAM) assume?"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "obtainableFactor at 0.01 overstates the realistic Year-1 capture by 2–3x; the defensible point sits at or below the band floor",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The node was already corrected once (3%→1% in refinement cycle 1) and carries maturity 'triangulated' — it reads as the fixed, conservative number. The residual error is not the value but the time-semantics of the sources it cites, which no band edge encodes.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A comparable early-stage DC-hardware entrant (intelligent PDU or adjacent rack power gear) documented capturing ≥1% of its serviceable market within its first 12 calendar months from launch.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.5,
  "rationale": "Three of four benchmark sources in the digest explicitly frame the percentage as multi-year, and the single worked example sits 5x below the band floor; the offsetting uncertainty is that the venture may have pre-launch pipeline the model doesn't credit.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.004,
   "note": "Pro-rated Year-1 capture consistent with cumulative-window benchmarks and the 0.1% worked example"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Months from launch to first booked CE purchase order",
   "where": "Own CRM plus TED (EU public tender portal) responses to rack-power lots the venture bids",
   "threshold": "No PO by month 6 post-launch",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "First-year revenue disclosures of comparable rack-power/DC-hardware entrants",
   "where": "Crunchbase/annual filings of recent PDU or rack-infrastructure startups entering EU",
   "threshold": "Comparables clustering below 0.5% of their stated SAM in year 1",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Build a bottom-up pipeline model (named accounts × win rate × cycle time × months of selling window) to replace the top-down 1% before fundraising commitments are set against YAM",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "Directly replaces the contested benchmark with venture-specific arithmetic"
  },
  {
   "action": "Re-baseline the internal plan on a 24-month obtainable window with quarterly gates, so Year-1 misses don't cascade into a credibility problem with the board",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "execution-window",
 "riskId": "risk.execution-window.som-window-semantics-year1-zero"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

But the benchmark corpus it leans on defines SOM as a 1–3 year (Prospeo, IdeaPlan) or 3–5 year (Wise) cumulative capture, not a Year-1 run-rate, and the one worked example in the corpus lands at 0.1% of SAM — 5x below the band's own floor. A 'conservative ramp' that books its first PO in month 7–9 doesn't earn 1% of SAM; it earns a fraction of the back half of the year. The residual error is not the value but the time-semantics of the sources it cites, which no band edge encodes.","expectedObviousness":"medium","falsifier":"A comparable early-stage DC-hardware entrant (intelligent PDU or adjacent rack power gear) documented capturing ≥1% of its serviceable market within its first 12 calendar months from launch.","likelihood":{"value":0.5,"rationale":"Three of four benchmark sources in the digest explicitly frame the percentage as multi-year, and the single worked example sits 5x below the band floor; the offsetting uncertainty is that the venture may have pre-launch pipeline the model doesn't credit.","basis":"evidence"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"obtainableFactor","op":"set","value":0.004,"note":"Pro-rated Year-1 capture consistent with cumulative-window benchmarks and the 0.1% worked example"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Months from launch to first booked CE purchase order","where":"Own CRM plus TED (EU public tender portal) responses to rack-power lots the venture bids","threshold":"No PO by month 6 post-launch","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"First-year revenue disclosures of comparable rack-power/DC-hardware entrants","where":"Crunchbase/annual filings of recent PDU or rack-infrastructure startups entering EU","threshold":"Comparables clustering below 0.5% of their stated SAM in year 1","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Build a bottom-up pipeline model (named accounts × win rate × cycle time × months of selling window) to replace the top-down 1% before fundraising commitments are set against YAM","type":"information","voiNodeId":"obtainableFactor","note":"Directly replaces the contested benchmark with venture-specific arithmetic"},{"action":"Re-baseline the internal plan on a 24-month obtainable window with quarterly gates, so Year-1 misses don't cascade into a credibility problem with the board","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
